title: Terminal tinkering after dinner
story: Most weeknights end with tea, a terminal window, and one tiny problem to solve. I keep the scope intentionally small so I can feel the full loop in one sitting: write it, run it, refine it.
takeaway: Small loops keep the hobby fun and momentum high.

title: Chasing a tone at midnight
story: Late at night I usually start with one guitar note and a reverb tail, then see where it goes. The best sessions happen when I stop aiming for a finished track and just follow whatever texture feels alive.

media.alt: Music desk with keys and ambient lighting

title: Weekly learning notes
story: I keep a lightweight notes ritual every weekend: one concept, one practical example, one open question. It helps me keep curiosity structured without turning free time into homework.
takeaway: A tiny cadence beats occasional deep dives.

title: Micro-interaction motion lab
story: I spent a weekend comparing easing curves on the same button transition to understand why some animations feel calm and others feel noisy. It was mostly visual trial-and-error with a lot of side-by-side toggling.
takeaway: Subtle motion improves feel more than feature count.

title: Sample/patch of the week
story: I started collecting one sound per week that I actually love, then writing a short note about why it works. This became a better habit than endlessly downloading presets I never revisit.

title: Typography rabbit hole
story: A quick article on readability turned into a full rabbit hole on rhythm, spacing, and contrast. I now keep a tiny checklist for what to inspect before calling a text-heavy UI done.
takeaway: Readability is a system, not a single setting.

media.alt: Abstract pattern used as a visual reference board

title: Favorite tool of the month
story: I keep a short running list of one utility that made my workflow meaningfully smoother each month. The goal is not novelty; it is noticing what consistently removes friction.
takeaway: The best tools disappear into your process.

title: Live loop practice sessions
story: Next up is a monthly 30-minute session where I build a loop from scratch in one take. No perfection pass, no over-editing, just committing to decisions and enjoying the moment.

media.alt: Cinematic studio scene for creative practice

title: Build a playful terminal toy
story: I want to ship a tiny scripted terminal interaction that tells a story in a few commands. It is a fun way to blend interface writing, pacing, and a bit of personality.
takeaway: Playful constraints make stronger UI storytelling.

title: Create a personal field guide
story: I am planning a one-page field guide for recurring lessons from side experiments: what surprised me, what broke, and what I would do differently next time.
takeaway: Reflection turns experiments into reusable knowledge.
